'''Ab'Bshingh''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], home to the [[Farang]] and [[Waroot]] [[Species/Legends|species]], who [[Ab'Bshingh civil war|fought]] each other continuously for [[Standard year/Legends|centuries]]. At some point, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] strategist [[Crat Dakerno]] went to the planet to study the two native species.

==Description==
Ab'Bshingh was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Ab'Bshingh system/Legends|Ab'Bshingh system]], within the [[Sujimis sector/Legends|Sujimis sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], that was discovered circa [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]] by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="Atlas" />

==History==
Ab'Bshingh was the site an enduring [[Ab'Bshingh civil war|civil war]] between the planet's native rivaling [[Farang]] and [[Waroot]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. Sometime during the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] reign, the Imperial [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] strategist [[Crat Dakerno]] traveled with a [[Troop/Legends|troop]] contingent to Ab'Bshingh, where he studied the two native species. Drawing on their tribal rituals and [[Standard year/Legends|centuries]]-old tactics, Dakerno created the [[holo boardgame]] [[B'shingh]].<ref name="ACPV" />

==Inhabitants==
The planet was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the Farang and Waroot species, who often fought complex battles, waging war against each other for centuries.<ref name="ACPV" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ab'Bshingh first appeared in the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 8|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 8]] short story "[[A Certain Point of View (short story)|A Certain Point of View]]," written by [[Charlene Newcomb]] in [[1995]]. [[1997|Two years later]], the planet was again mentioned in ''[[Gundark's Fantastic Technology: Personal Gear]]'', a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] supplement published by [[West End Games]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', and in [[2008]], the planet received a brief entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.
